layout.workspaceDescription: Your AI workspace — chat, skills, integrations, memory, and runtime controls in one place.
layout.sidebarFooter: Chat, skills, integrations, profiles, and settings share one workspace shell. Chat history stays separate so the UI feels like a workspace, not a page list.

nav.marketplaceDescription: Discover skills, MCP servers, and plugins before you install them
nav.skillsDescription: Reusable procedures, references, and local skill edits
nav.integrationsDescription: Live integrations, MCP servers, available tools, approvals, and diagnostics
nav.pluginsDescription: Repo-based extensions for custom hooks and bundled tools
nav.memoryDescription: User memory, agent memory, context, and chat history
nav.profilesDescription: Active profile, defaults, policies, and access

topbar.workspaceDescription: Chat, tools, approvals, skills, and runtime context stay visible in one place.
topbar.skillsDescription: Browse, inspect, and curate skills without losing runtime visibility.
topbar.extensionsDescription: See extension health, auth posture, and runtime compatibility at a glance.
topbar.memoryDescription: Understand what the agent remembers and what context is active in chat.

login.title: A calmer AI workspace for real work
login.subtitle: Pan keeps chat, runtime visibility, memory, and installable capabilities in one place so you can move from question to execution without losing context.
login.mentalModelDescription: Start in Chat for the actual task, use Marketplace to discover what to add, open Integrations for live availability, and use Plugins only when you need deeper runtime customization.
login.signInDescription: Access your workspace for chat, memory, runtime visibility, and installable capabilities.
login.secureAccessDescription: Sign in with the credentials configured for this Pan instance. If you do not have access yet, ask the workspace owner or administrator.
login.errorBothFields: Enter both your username and password to continue.
login.whatYouWillSeeDescription: Chat for the task itself, Marketplace for discovery, Integrations for live tool availability, Plugins for deeper runtime extensions, and Profiles for separate defaults and memory.
login.skillsDescription: Reusable procedures, references, and templates you can load into a chat when the task needs structure.
login.mcpServersDescription: Live tool connections that let the agent reach external systems and actions.
login.pluginsDescription: Repo-based runtime extensions for deeper hooks, packaged tools, and custom behavior.
login.profilesDescription: Separate defaults, memory, and runtime posture so different contexts stay cleanly isolated.

drawer.overviewDescription: Current context, loaded skills, and memory that shape the next reply.
drawer.activityDescription: What the agent is doing right now, including approvals and recent events.
drawer.toolsDescription: Which tools and runtime surfaces are active in this session.
drawer.outputDescription: Artifacts and sources emitted while the run unfolds.
drawer.currentContextDescription: The main ingredients shaping the next answer.
drawer.startChatting: Start chatting to build a richer working context.
drawer.loadedSkillsDescription: Reusable procedures and references already attached to this conversation.
drawer.noSkillsAttached: No explicit skills are attached yet. Open Skills when you want reusable procedures or references.
drawer.memorySnippetsDescription: Compact recall currently visible to the agent.
drawer.noMemorySnippets: No memory snippets surfaced for this session yet.
drawer.whatIsHappeningDescription: Use this tab when you want the agent's live status without leaving the chat.
drawer.approvalQueueDescription: High-visibility requests that need human confirmation.
drawer.toolTimelineDescription: Latest tool and phase events emitted by the agent.
drawer.noActivityYet: No activity yet. Start a run to populate the timeline.
drawer.toolPostureDescription: What kinds of actions the current run is using and how risky they are.
drawer.seenToolsDescription: Quick inventory of tool families already used in this session.
drawer.noToolsExecuted: No tools have executed yet for this session.
drawer.connectedRuntimeDescription: Runtime-provided MCP servers and execution surfaces.
drawer.noMcpServers: No MCP servers reported by the runtime.
drawer.generatedOutputDescription: Artifacts, plans, files, and structured content emitted during the run.
drawer.sourcesAndCitationsDescription: Retrieved pages, references, and snippets surfaced by the agent.
drawer.noSourcesEmitted: No sources have been emitted for this session yet.
drawer.threadSummaryDescription: Core metadata for the active conversation thread.
drawer.title: Title
drawer.threadSettingsDescription: Runtime behavior selected for this conversation.
drawer.workspaceLinkageDescription: How this thread relates to the broader workspace.
drawer.forkedFrom: Derived from {id}. Forking preserves the old run while letting you branch.
drawer.primarySession: This session is the main line for its current workspace context.

chat.runtimeUnavailableDescription: You can still inspect saved chats, approvals, sources, and artifacts, but sending new messages is paused until the backend reconnects.
chat.runtimeOffline: Runtime offline — sending is paused until it reconnects.
chat.mockModeActive: Mock mode active · Enter to send · Shift+Enter for newline · drag files to attach.
chat.startWithRequest: Start with one request. You can add files or open details if the task needs more context.
chat.enterToSend: Enter to send · Shift+Enter for newline · drag files to attach.
chat.mockModeBanner: Mock mode is active for chat flows, approvals, sources, and artifacts.
chat.archiveSessionDescription: Archive this conversation but keep it available in history and search.
chat.deleteSessionDescription: This permanently removes the selected conversation from the current chat history.
chat.runtimeApiUnavailable: Message saved, but the runtime API is unavailable right now. Check diagnostics or retry when the backend comes back.
chat.starterPrompt1: Summarize this context and give me the best next step
chat.starterPrompt2: Help me turn the current task into an implementation plan
chat.starterPrompt3: Review the current context and point out risks or open questions
chat.talkingToProvider: Talking to {provider} via the live runtime.
chat.mockModeDescription: Mock mode is active for chat flows, approvals, sources, and artifacts.
chat.runtimeDetectedUnreachable: Runtime is detected but the API is unreachable, so sending is paused until connectivity returns.
chat.runtimeNotDetected: Runtime was not detected. The UI can still show saved local state and explicit fallback labels.

sessions.noChatsFoundDescription: Try a different search term or start a new chat.
sessions.noSourceChatsDescription: Change the source filter or start a new chat.

skills.title: Skills
skills.searchSkills: Search skills by name, tag, or category…
skills.noSkillsMatch: No skills match "{query}". Try a different filter.
skills.noInstalledSkills: No installed skills were detected for the current runtime/profile scope.
skills.installedCount: Browse {count} installed skills across {categories} categories. {enabled} enabled. {withFiles} include references, scripts, or templates.
skills.discoverDescription: Discover skills from skills.sh and other registries. {available} available to install{total}.
skills.securityScanBlockedDescription: {name} comes from a community source and triggered a caution verdict. Pan blocked the default install to avoid silently pulling in risky skills.
skills.forceInstallDescription: If you trust this skill and understand the findings above, you can force the install once. Otherwise, cancel and pick a different skill.
skills.noSkillsFound: No skills found for "{query}". Try a different search term.
skills.noDiscoverableSkills: No discoverable skills found. The skills.sh cache may need to be populated — run `hermes skills browse` in your terminal.
skills.installInProgress: A skill install is in progress. Pan temporarily locks the other install buttons so you do not fire overlapping installs and end up with stale cards.

extensions.title: Integrations
extensions.subtitle: Understand what is installed, what the agent can use right now, and what still needs setup. MCP servers live here; repo-based plugins stay on the separate Plugins page.
extensions.installedNowDescription: Connected integrations across built-in and MCP sources.
extensions.availableToolsDescription: Tools the agent can reach from the current workspace context.
extensions.needsAttentionDescription: Integrations that are degraded, blocked, or missing auth.
extensions.whatIsIntegrationDescription: An integration is a connected capability Pan can inspect and the agent can call. MCP servers show up here because they expose live tools.
extensions.whatAreToolsDescription: Tools are the actions the agent can actually run. Some are global, some follow the active profile, and some only apply to the current chat.
extensions.wherePluginsFitDescription: Plugins are deeper repo-based extensions. They can add hooks and tool bundles, but they are managed separately so this page can stay focused on live availability.
extensions.installedIntegrationsDescription: Everything already connected for this workspace, including built-in and MCP-backed integrations.
extensions.installedMcpServersDescription: Only the MCP servers already configured for the active profile.
extensions.discoverMcpServersDescription: Browse more MCP servers to install without mixing them into the current inventory.
extensions.toolsAgentCanUseDescription: This view explains what is currently available everywhere, by profile, or only in this chat.
extensions.approvalRulesDescription: See which integrations are blocked, approval-gated, or limited by policy before a tool can run.
extensions.diagnosticsDescription: Inspect runtime health when discovery or live checks look incomplete or degraded.
extensions.availableEverywhereDescription: Tools that do not depend on profile or chat scope.
extensions.followsProfileDescription: Changes when you switch the active profile.
extensions.onlyThisChatDescription: Limited to the active chat for safer experimentation.
extensions.onlyThisChatNoSession: These appear once a chat-level tool is attached.
extensions.runtimeDataDegradedDescription: Some integration information is still readable, but live runtime checks are unavailable right now.
extensions.couldNotLoadDescription: Pan could not read the current integrations state from the runtime.
extensions.noMcpServersDescription: Add an MCP server directly or browse the MCP Hub to connect one.
extensions.noIntegrationsDescription: This profile has no connected integrations yet. Start with an MCP server, then inspect tools and approvals once it is live.
extensions.noApprovalRules: No extra approval rules right now
extensions.noApprovalRulesDescription: No installed integrations currently need explicit approval handling beyond the default runtime posture.

addMcpServer.title: Add MCP server
addMcpServer.description: Create a new MCP extension by command or URL.
addMcpServer.nameAndCommandRequired: Name plus command or URL is required.

mcpInstall.title: Install MCP Server
mcpInstall.configureEnv: Configure required environment variables for this server.

memory.title: Memory
memory.subtitle: Inspect prompt memory, search prior sessions, and review current context state.
memory.userMemoryDescription: Durable facts about the human on the other side: preferences, identity, and stable habits worth carrying into future sessions.
memory.agentMemoryDescription: Operational notes about the environment, conventions, and workflow details that help the agent behave consistently later.
memory.editingIsDurableDescription: Changes are saved immediately and affect future sessions. The current session context remains what was already loaded unless you start a fresh thread.
memory.sessionSearchNotMemoryDescription: Search lets you recover prior conversations without promoting them into persistent memory.

plugins.title: Plugins
plugins.subtitle: Plugins are repo-based extensions for custom hooks and bundled tools. Use MCP servers for live external systems, and use plugins when you need deeper runtime customization.
plugins.noPluginsDescription: Plugins are optional and usually come after skills and MCP servers. Install one when you need repo-based hooks, tool bundles, or runtime customization.
plugins.howPluginsDifferDescription: Integrations and MCP servers focus on what the agent can reach right now. Plugins focus on adding custom hooks, packaged tools, and deeper behavior changes to the runtime.
plugins.loadingDescription: Reading installed plugins and their tool/hook metadata for this workspace.
plugins.couldNotLoadDescription: Pan could not read the installed plugin inventory right now.

profiles.title: Profiles
profiles.subtitle: Each profile is its own workspace boundary. Keep identity, policy, model defaults, and integration trust visible before you start a run.
profiles.selectedPostureDescription: The UI selection and the runtime-selected profile should match. This panel makes any drift obvious.
profiles.profileRequeryDescription: Sessions, skills, extensions, memory context, approvals, and diagnostics are invalidated together when you activate a new profile.
profiles.policyPresetsDefaults: Policy presets are defaults, not hidden magic
profiles.policyPresetsDefaultsDescription: Every preset influences tool approvals, memory behavior, and extension access. The rest of the workspace should reflect the same selected profile immediately.

settings.cockpitDescription: Operate with explicit visibility into runtime state, policy posture, diagnostics, and durable history.
settings.runtimePostureDescription: This panel tells you whether the runtime is live, which profile is active, and what the workspace can safely do right now.
settings.activeProfilePolicyDescription: The selected profile controls memory scope, default guardrails, and which integrations are visible across the workspace.
settings.operationalBrowsersDescription: Dedicated operator views for approvals, runs, telemetry, audit, diagnostics, and generated outputs.
settings.browserApprovalsDescription: Review blocked actions, reasons, and operator decisions.
settings.browserRunsDescription: Inspect durable run state, phases, artifacts, and failures.
settings.browserTelemetryDescription: Trace client and server events for debugging and product insight.
settings.browserAuditDescription: Search persistent governance and configuration changes.
settings.browserHealthDescription: Check runtime, provider, memory, and MCP remediation signals.
settings.browserMcpDiagnosticsDescription: Validate MCP servers, discovered tools, and probe failures.
settings.browserArtifactsDescription: Browse files, plans, and outputs emitted by prior runs.

capabilityLabels.loadedOnlyInSession: Loaded only in the active session
